entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/ci-management-jjb-verify/29/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/ci-management-jjb-verify/29

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-2

Do not create a new change to repair problems with a previous submission issue that is failing CI. Instead, you should push an updated version of the change.

The standard workflow with gerrit goes like this (assuming you've already cloned and are using the git-review plugin)

git checkout master && git pull
git checkout -b topic1
# make changes
git commit -as
# give a good commit message
git review

# go work on something else
git checkout master && git pull
git checkout -b topic2
# make changes
git commit -as
# good commit message
git review

# topic1 failed build or changes need to be made
git checkout master && git pull && git checkout topic1
git rebase master
# make fixes
git commit -as --amend
# the amend is important! update the commit message if needed
git review

# topic2 is fine and got merged
git checkout master && git pull && git branch -D topic2

Abandoned

Meant to submit change to earlier change
